questions.question:

(ISRO CSE 2014)

questions.question:

{{E,F}\(\rightarrow\){G}, {F}\(\rightarrow\){I,J}, {E,H}\(\rightarrow\){K,L}, {K}\(\rightarrow\){M}, {L}\(\rightarrow\){N} } on R. What is the key for R?

(GATE CSE 2014 SET-1)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider a relational table with a single record for each registered student with the following attributes.

1. Registration_Number: Unique registration number for each registered student
2. UID: Unique Identity number, unique at the national level for each citizen
3. BankAccount_Number: Unique account number at the bank. A student can have multiple accounts or joint accounts. This attributes stores the primary account number
4. Name: Name of the Student
5. Hostel_Room: Room number of the hostel

Which of the following options is INCORRECT?

(GATE CSE 2011)
options:
C.

UID is a candidate key if all students are from the same country

D.

If \(S\) is a superkey such that \(\text{S}\cap \text{UID}\) is NULL then \(\text{S}\cup \text{UID}\) is also a superkey
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
